title: Swamithope Pathi
tagline: Swamithope Pathi — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Swamithoppu Pathi (Tamil: சுவாமிதோப்பு பதி, Swamithoppu-pathi, Manavai-pathi, Detchana-pathi, or Thamarai-pathi) is the primary pathi of the Ayyavazhi and the sacred venue of the T
overview.description: Swamithoppu Pathi (Tamil: சுவாமிதோப்பு பதி, Swamithoppu-pathi, Manavai-pathi, Detchana-pathi, or Thamarai-pathi) is the primary pathi of the Ayyavazhi and the sacred venue of the Tavam. Swamithope is considered primary among the Pancha pathi and the primary centre of the incarnational activities of Vaikundar. According to the Ayyavazhi legends, Lord Vaikundar, an incarnation of Lord Narayana, carried out his tavam at Poovantanthoppe, attracting many followers across India. Upon his death, Lord Vaikundar is believed to have been interred there, with the square-shaped 'pathi' constructed to enve

topPlaces.name: Muthiri kinaru
topPlaces.description: Muthiri Kinaru (Tamil: முத்திரி கிணறு) is the sacred well located in the north-western corner of Swamithoppe village. This is the famous theertha of the temple.

topPlaces.name: Thenthamaraikulam
topPlaces.description: Thenthamaraikulam is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Swamithope
topPlaces.description: Swamithope (alternate spelling Swamithoppe) is the name of a village that lies southeast of the City of Nagercoil, the capital of the District of Kanyakumari in the State of Tamil Nadu, at the extreme southern tip of India. In the past, Swamithope was known by the name Poovandanthoppe which was part of the village, Sasthankutty Vilai Swamithope lies about half-way between the cities of Nagercoil and Kanniyakumari on the Nagercoil-Kanniyakumari road.

topPlaces.name: Thamaraikulam Pathi
topPlaces.description: Thamaraikulam Pathi (Tamil: தாமரைகுளம் பதி) is one among the Pancha pathi, the five holy places of Ayyavazhi. The Ari Gopalan Citar, who wrote the Akilam, was born here.

topPlaces.name: Marunthuvazh Malai
topPlaces.description: The Marundhuvazh Malai, a part of Sanjeevi hills is also known as the Marundhu Vazhum Malai/Maruthwamalai ("the abode of medicinal herbs"), forms the part and the southernmost tip of the Western Ghats in Agasteeswaram taluk of Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India. People living in southern Kerala call it "Maruthuva Mala".

topPlaces.name: Puthalam
topPlaces.description: Puththalam is a Panchayat town in the Agastheeswaram taluk, Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It lies between the Manakudy estuary and the Indian Ocean.

topPlaces.name: Kulasekarapuram
topPlaces.description: Kulasekarapuram is a village is near to the Osaravillai in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Atchenkulam
topPlaces.description: Atchenkulam is a village in Kanyakumari District, Tamil Nadu. The village is 1 km from Kottaram, part of Kottaram town Panchayat and comes under Agastheeswaram taluk.

items.note: Muthiri Kinaru (Tamil: முத்திரி கிணறு) is the sacred well located in the north-western corner of Swamithoppe village. This is the famous theertha of the temple.
Thenthamaraikulam is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Swamithope (alternate spelling Swamithoppe) is the name of a village that lies southeast of the City of Nagercoil, the capital of the District of Kanyakumari in the State of Tamil Nadu, at the extreme southern tip of India. In the past, Swamithope was known by the name Poovandanthoppe which was part of the village, Sasthankutty Vilai Swamithope lies about half-way between the cities of Nagercoil and Kanniyakumari on the Nagercoil-Kanniyakumari road.
Thamaraikulam Pathi (Tamil: தாமரைகுளம் பதி) is one among the Pancha pathi, the five holy places of Ayyavazhi. The Ari Gopalan Citar, who wrote the Akilam, was born here.

The Marundhuvazh Malai, a part of Sanjeevi hills is also known as the Marundhu Vazhum Malai/Maruthwamalai ("the abode of medicinal herbs"), forms the part and the southernmost tip of the Western Ghats in Agasteeswaram taluk of Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India. People living in southern Kerala call it "Maruthuva Mala".
Puththalam is a Panchayat town in the Agastheeswaram taluk, Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It lies between the Manakudy estuary and the Indian Ocean.
Kulasekarapuram is a village is near to the Osaravillai in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
Atchenkulam is a village in Kanyakumari District, Tamil Nadu. The village is 1 km from Kottaram, part of Kottaram town Panchayat and comes under Agastheeswaram taluk.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Swamithope Pathi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Swamithope Pathi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Swamithope Pathi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (75 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Swamithope Pathi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Swamithope Pathi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Swamithope Pathi?
faq.a: Stays in Swamithope Pathi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Swamithope Pathi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Muthiri kinaru, Thenthamaraikulam, Swamithope, Thamaraikulam Pathi, Marunthuvazh Malai.
